# Mid-Level Data Scientist
...
Vilnius, Lithuania
...
June 10, 2026
...
### Job Description
...
As a Mid-Level Data Scientist reporting to the Director of Data Science, you’ll play a meaningful role in crafting solutions, translating data into meaningful insights, and helping build the future of our products and client experiences.
...
You’ll thrive
...
Key Responsibilities
...
- Design, research, and develop machine learning and AI solutions that drive measurable business outcomes across the enterprise.
- Translate data insights into practical, decision-ready recommendations for both technical and non-technical collaborators.
- Partner with key business collaborators to understand their objectives and communicate the value and impact of data science initiatives.
...
- Provide regular updates on project progress, findings, and potential impact on key performance indicators.
...
- Foster a collaborative, informed decision-making culture by presenting insights clearly and accessibly across teams.
...
- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience in Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Statistics, or a related technical field.
- 2-4 years of experience as a Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, or in a similar role.
- Solid understanding of statistical, machine learning, and deep learning fundamentals, including hypothesis testing, regression, classification, optimization, and core neural network concepts and architectures.
- Proficiency in Python and SQL or equivalent relational database technologies.
...
- Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to guide cross-functional teams on complex, data-driven projects.
...
- Experience in a corporate, finance, or fintech environment.
- Hands-on experience with generative AI tools and large language model workflows.
- Practical industry experience with end-to-end deep learning model development, from problem definition through evaluation and monitoring.

...
As a Product Owner reporting to the Sr. Manager of Listings Product Management, you'll play a critical role in shaping innovative, AI-enabled solutions for regulated client-facing platforms and internal tools within Nasdaq's Listings organization.
...
You'll thrive in this position if you're collaborative, data-driven, and passionate about leveraging technology to improve workflows and deliver exceptional user experiences in a fast-paced financial services environment.
...
* Own and prioritize product backlogs, define user stories, and lead sprint planning, backlog grooming, and stakeholder reviews to deliver high-quality features in collaboration with cross-functional scrum teams.
...
* Design and reimagine end-to-end workflows to optimize efficiency, usability, and regulatory compliance across client-facing and internal platforms — including developing and maintaining product roadmaps aligned with organizational objectives.
...
* Identify, envision, and implement AI-driven solutions that enhance product capabilities, streamline processes, and improve user experiences; stay current with AI developments relevant to product management and financial services.
...
**Required Qualifications
...
* 3+ years of experience

product ownership, or a related role.

...

...

Excited to share the release of my new whitepaper: “Future Ready Markets – Preparing Data for AI Applications”—a deep dive into how AI is reshaping the financial markets and what it takes to be truly AI-ready.

 

From high-quality, accessible datasets to the right talent and culture, I explore the foundational elements that empower exchanges and clearinghouses to unlock AI’s full potential. Drawing on lessons from Nasdaq 's own journey, this paper offers a practical roadmap for market operators.

 

One of the central themes: how Nasdaq Eqlipse Intelligence—a cloud-native, modular data management solution, has already powered breakthrough AI innovations at Nasdaq and can help financial market infrastructures (FMIs) modernize operations and scale AI initiatives.

 

Whether you're a data scientist, technologist or exchange leader, I hope this paper helps you navigate the path to AI readiness.

 

Check out the whitepaper to explore how to turn data challenges into opportunities: https://lnkd.in/etSCMDvb

results.title: Kristina Kasačiūnaitė
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist at [Nasdaq](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nasdaq)
...
Hi, I'm Kristina — a Senior Data Scientist at Nasdaq with a PhD in Biochemistry and a career path that proves the best transitions are the unexpected ones. My professional journey began in the life sciences. I spent years at the research bench — cloning DNA, designing experiments with magnetic tweezers, and publishing peer-reviewed work on helicases and DNA repair mechanisms. What I discovered along the way was that the part of science I found most compelling wasn't the wet lab — it was the data. The process of transforming raw observations into structured, actionable insight is what ultimately drew me into data science, and it continues to drive my work today. At Nasdaq, I operate at the intersection of artificial intelligence and financial markets. Our team works across a broad spectrum of methodologies — classical machine learning, deep learning, and large language models — delivering solutions for both internal stakeholders and external clients. I'm particularly motivated by complex, multi-disciplinary projects that demand depth and rigor, not just surface-level implementations. Prior to Nasdaq, I spent over two years at Unisys, where my role as a data analyst extended well beyond the title. I built data pipelines, implemented CI/CD workflows, applied machine learning models, and worked across textual, categorical, and numerical data within Databricks. That experience established a strong foundation in production-grade data engineering and analytics. Between career chapters, I took parental leave — an experience that refined skills no professional development course could replicate: prioritization under pressure, rapid context-switching, and effective negotiation with a decidedly uncooperative stakeholder. It was humbling, rewarding, and surprisingly transferable. My scientific training — hypothesis-driven thinking, experimental rigor, and comfort navigating ambiguity — remains central to how I approach every data problem. While I've moved on from the bench, that foundation informs everything I do. I'm not actively seeking new opportunities — I'm genuinely engaged in my current work. That said, I'm always open to connecting, exchanging perspectives on AI in finance, or discussing unconventional career paths. Feel free to reach out.
...

My expertise spans developing innovative AI solutions and algorithms, significantly contributing to advancements in image processing, generation, and recognition. Industry Experience: 1. I’m a Data Scientist and AI Engineer with a strong mix of deep academic training and real-world industry success. With three master’s degrees—in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, and Engineering—and over four years of hands-on experience, I’ve moved beyond theory to deliver real, measurable impact. From multiple peer-reviewed publications to more than 50 specialized certifications, I stay at the forefront of the field and continuously push the boundaries of what’s possible. My expertise spans the entire AI pipeline. I’ve built and deployed sophisticated machine learning models, tackled large-scale data ingestion and modeling, and streamlined end-to-end MLOps for continuous improvement and reliability. I also have a track record of translating complex insights into actionable strategies using leading BI tools (Tableau, Looker). results.title: Growth Marketing Specialist, Data Business | Hirevector
highlights: Data business in our Capital Access Platforms division. This role is an opportunity for a data-driven growth marketer to establish and grow her/his career at Nasdaq and play a key role in helping to drive growth in Nasdaq’s flagship Data business. In this role, you will collaborate closely with colleagues across Marketing, Sales and Product to deliver lead generation campaigns that drive high-quality opportunities into the sales pipeline to support the business’s revenue goals. You will collaborate closely with our Co-Marketing, Product Marketing, Performance Marketing, Events and Campaign Center of Excellence functions. This role will offer you a rich opportunity to learn Nasdaq’s critical financial data and technology business while meaningfully applying your marketing skills. You will develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns that incorporate the full range of marketing tactics, including email, content, paid and organic search, digital advertising and social, as well as live and virtual events, competitor research and legal submissions. Responsibilities: Work closely with colleagues across Marketing to design and implement integrated marketing strategies to drive client and prospect engagement around a range of financial data and technology products and events Plan, organize, and manage multiple concurrent marketing campaigns across different lifecycle stages, tactics, and priorities,—ensuring alignment to business goals, clear timelines, and seamless execution across channels, teams, and stakeholders. Develop and execute Account Based Marketing (ABM) strategies to drive targeted engagement and business growth across our Data products and services Develop email marketing campaigns using Marketo, Outreach and Salesforce in collaboration with Sales and Product teams and our Campaign Center of Excellence Help develop content necessary for communicating marketing strategy internally as well as material needed for marketing campaigns including emails, presentations, product collateral, search ads, social posts, webinars/videos and website content. Leverage AI, data, and martech capabilities to analyze customer behavior, design and execute strategic A/B tests, and translate insights into actionable personalization strategies. Own the design of scalable, automated nurture and always‑on campaigns that optimize engagement, conversion, and lifecycle performance across channels. Partner closely with the Director of Co-Marketing to execute Nasdaq branded co-marketing campaigns and on-site executions Collaborate closely with colleagues and leverage the martech stack to effectively report on the impact of marketing campaigns to business partners Conduct competitive research and help maintain our competitive database on Salesforce in collaboration with Product Marketing team Requirements: Educational background in Marketing and/or Communications preferred Experience Required: 3-5 years of experience in marketing, strong preference toward financial services and/or fintech experience Strong knowledge of marketing, Office, CMS experience and analytics tools such as Marketo, Monday.com, Salesforce, Google Analytics, PowerPoint, Excel, Word, etc. Strong written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of marketing strategies and key metrics Come as You Are Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, color, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation. What We Offer

Exciting times at the Chase Delaware Technology Center! We just wrapped up our first Agentic AI workshop of 2026, and the energy and engagement from everyone involved was inspiring. 
 
This hands-on session gave our teams the chance to explore Agentic AI systems and orchestrate intelligent agents—skills that are rapidly becoming essential in today’s technology landscape. By equipping our technologists with the latest tools and practical experience, we’re not only driving innovation and efficiency within Chase but also building solutions that have a direct impact on our customers and frontline teams. The highlight? Every participant successfully built and ran their own use case, demonstrating how accessible and transformative these technologies can be. 
 
At Chase , collaborative learning is at the core of our technology culture. Events like this go beyond sharing knowledge. They create an environment where technologists at every level can experiment, ask questions and learn by doing. These hands-on experiences break down barriers, spark new ideas and ensure that everyone from newcomers to seasoned experts can grow together and contribute to meaningful progress. 
 
Thank you to everyone who contributed, presented and participated, especially those who braved the snowy weather to make this event a success! Your curiosity and commitment keep us moving forward.

results.title: Analyst - Economic & Statistical Research | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
# Analyst - Economic & Statistical Research
...
Through statistical analysis and research, Nasdaq Economic and Statistical Research provides management with key data responsible for a variety of projects and initiatives to support the Nasdaq market and improve its market structure. As an Analyst in our Economic and Statistical Research (‘ER’) department, reporting to the Director of Economic Research, you will play a critical role in delivering high quality insights by providing highly quantitative, analytical expertise to all Nasdaq business lines, locations, and levels of management. You will partner with key stakeholders across the organization to inform business and product decisions and learn about Nasdaq’s broad array of products. ER Analysts become experts in quantitative analysis, analytics, pricing, financial products, market microstructure, financial regulation, and exchange competition. ER analysts have frequent contact with all levels of management, customers, and regulators, providing excellent opportunities for advancement. You will thrive in this position if you are analytical, data-driven, collaborative, inquisitive, have a global mindset, and bring a passion for finance, data, and communicating insights to a fast-paced, high-impact environment. Some of the topics the team works on can be found on Nasdaq’s Market Makers blog. This position will primarily support pricing related work for Nasdaq’s North American Markets group. This will include modeling pricing changes, verifying and auditing billing systems, and providing analytical support for product stakeholders.
